18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs

21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		Once         bool

32		WorkerID     string
33		ProjectRoot  string
34	}

36	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per

38	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.

42		BeadID      string `json:"bead_id"`
43		AttemptID   string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`

55		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
56		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
57		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
58		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
59		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
60		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for

62		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
63		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).

93	// SatisfactionChecker evaluates whether a bead that returned no_changes is
94	// already satisfied and should be closed, or is still unresolved and should
95	// receive retry suppression. noChangesCount is the cumulative count including
96	// the current attempt.
97	//
98	// When satisfied is true the caller closes the bead with the returned evidence
99	// string as the detail. When false the caller applies a retry cooldown and
100	// leaves the bead open.
101	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
102		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (satisfied bool, evidence string, err error)
103	}
104	
105	// SatisfactionCheckerFunc is a functional adapter for SatisfactionChecker.
106	type SatisfactionCheckerFunc func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
107	
108	func (f SatisfactionCheckerFunc) CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error) {
109		return f(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
110	}

180	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
181	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
182	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
183	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
184		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
185		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
186		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
187		Now                 func() time.Time
188		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
189		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
190		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
191		Reviewer BeadReviewer
192	}

194	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
195	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
196	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
197		if ch == nil {
198			return
199		}
200		select {
201		case ch <- evt:
202		default:
203		}
204	}

206	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
207	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
208		return ProgressEvent{
209			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
210			AttemptID: attemptID,
211			WorkerID:  workerID,
212			ProjectID: projectID,
213			BeadID:    beadID,
214			Harness:   harness,
215			Model:     model,
216			Profile:   profile,
217			Phase:     phase,
218			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
219			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
220			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
221			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
222		}
223	}
224	
225	func randomProgressID() string {
226		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
227	}

229	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
230		if w.Store == nil {
231			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
232		}
233		if w.Executor == nil {
234			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
235		}
236	
237		now := w.Now
238		if now == nil {
239			now = time.Now
240		}
241		assignee := rcfg.Assignee()
242		if assignee == "" {
243			assignee = "ddx"
244		}
245		noProgressCooldown := rcfg.NoProgressCooldown()
246		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
247			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
248		}
249		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := rcfg.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
250		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
251			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
252		}
253		heartbeatInterval := rcfg.HeartbeatInterval()
254		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
255			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
256		}
257		harness := rcfg.Harness()
258		model := rcfg.Model()
259		profile := rcfg.Profile()
260	
261		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
262		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
263	
264		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
265			writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
266		}
267	
268		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
269			"worker_id":    runtime.WorkerID,
270			"project_root": runtime.ProjectRoot,
271			"harness":      harness,
272			"model":        model,
273			"session_id":   runtime.SessionID,
274			"assignee":     assignee,
275			"once":         runtime.Once,
276		})
277		defer func() {
278			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
279				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
280				"successes":           result.Successes,
281				"failures":            result.Failures,
282				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
283			})
284		}()
285	
286		for {
287			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
288			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
289			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
290			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
291			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
292				return result, err
293			}
294	
295			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, runtime.LabelFilter)
296			if err != nil {
297				return result, err
298			}
299			if !ok {
300				if result.Attempts == 0 {
301					result.NoReadyWork = true
302					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
303						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
304							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
305								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
306								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
307								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
308								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
309								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
310							}
311						}
312					}
313				}
314				if runtime.PollInterval <= 0 {
315					return result, nil
316				}
317				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, runtime.PollInterval); err != nil {
318					return result, err
319				}
320				continue
321			}
322	
323			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
324	
325			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
326			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
327			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
328			if runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {
329				if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
330					if runtime.Log != nil {
331						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
332					}
333					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
334						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
335						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
336					})
337					continue
338				}
339			}
340	
341			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
342				continue
343			}
344	
345			emit("bead.claimed", map[string]any{
346				"bead_id":  candidate.ID,
347				"title":    candidate.Title,
348				"assignee": assignee,
349			})
350	
351			if runtime.Log != nil {
352				if candidate.Title != "" {
353					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s: %s\n", candidate.ID, candidate.Title)
354				} else {
355					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "\n▶ %s\n", candidate.ID)
356				}
357			}
358	
359			// Generate a provisional attempt_id for progress events.
360			// The real attempt_id is assigned inside ExecuteBead; we use this
361			// for queueing/running events and replace with the real one once known.
362			provAttemptID := time.Now().UTC().Format("20060102T150405") + "-" + randomProgressID()
363			runStart := now()
364			phaseSeq := 0
365			nextPhase := func(phase string, heartbeat bool) {
366				phaseSeq++
367				emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, newProgressEvent(
368					runtime.WorkerID, runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, provAttemptID,
369					harness, model, profile,
370					phase, phaseSeq, heartbeat, now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
371				))
372			}
373	
374			nextPhase("queueing", false)
375	
376			hbCtx, hbCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
377			var hbWG sync.WaitGroup
378			hbWG.Add(1)
379			go func(beadID string) {
380				defer hbWG.Done()
381				ticker := time.NewTicker(heartbeatInterval)
382				defer ticker.Stop()
383				for {
384					select {
385					case <-hbCtx.Done():
386						return
387					case <-ticker.C:
388						_ = w.Store.Heartbeat(beadID)
389					}
390				}
391			}(candidate.ID)
392	
393			nextPhase("running", false)
394	
395			report, err := w.Executor.Execute(ctx, candidate.ID)
396			hbCancel()
397			hbWG.Wait()
398			if err != nil {
399				report = ExecuteBeadReport{
400					BeadID: candidate.ID,
401					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
402					Detail: err.Error(),
403				}
404			}
405			if report.BeadID == "" {
406				report.BeadID = candidate.ID
407			}
408			if report.Status == "" {
409				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
410			}
411			if report.Detail == "" {
412				report.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(report.Status, "", "")
413			}
414	
415			result.Attempts++
416	
417			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
418				appendLoopRoutingEvidence(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, now().UTC())
419				// Close the bead early when review is skipped. The closure gate
420				// (ddx-e30e60a9) accepts this path because closing_commit_sha is
421				// set and there is no malformed-APPROVE event to reject.
422				reviewApproved := true
423				reviewSkipped := w.Reviewer == nil || runtime.NoReview || HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip")
424	
425				if reviewSkipped {
426					if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {
427						return result, err
428					}
429				}
430	
431				if w.Reviewer != nil && !runtime.NoReview && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, "review:skip") {
432					reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
433					if reviewErr != nil {
434						// FEAT-022 §12+§14: classify the failure into the four-class
435						// taxonomy and count prior review-error events scoped to the
436						// current result_rev. On the Nth failure emit a terminal
437						// review-manual-required event instead of yet another
438						// review-error, parking the bead so a subsequent loop
439						// iteration does NOT re-execute primary.
440						class := classifyReviewError(reviewErr, reviewRes)
441						prior := countPriorReviewErrors(w.Store, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev)
442						attemptCount := prior + 1
443						maxRetries := rcfg.ReviewMaxRetries()
444						if maxRetries <= 0 {
445							maxRetries = DefaultReviewMaxRetries
446						}
447						errSummary := EventBodySummary{
448							InputBytes:  0,
449							OutputBytes: 0,
450						}
451						if reviewRes != nil {
452							errSummary = EventBodySummary{
453								Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
454								Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
455								InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
456								OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
457								ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
458							}
459						}
460						if attemptCount >= maxRetries {
461							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
462							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
463								Kind:      "review-manual-required",
464								Summary:   class,
465								Body:      body,
466								Actor:     assignee,
467								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
468								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
469							})
470							// Park the bead with a long cooldown so subsequent
471							// loop iterations do NOT re-pick it for primary
472							// re-execution. Reviewer-failure invariant (§13) is
473							// preserved: the bead is NOT closed.
474							parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
475							_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, "review-manual-required", class)
476						} else {
477							body := appendEventSummary(reviewErrorEventBody(class, attemptCount, report.ResultRev, reviewErr.Error()), errSummary)
478							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
479								Kind:      "review-error",
480								Summary:   class,
481								Body:      body,
482								Actor:     assignee,
483								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
484								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
485							})
486						}
487					} else {
488						report.ReviewVerdict = string(reviewRes.Verdict)
489						report.ReviewRationale = reviewRes.Rationale
490						// Persist the full reviewer stream as an artifact so the
491						// event body never carries the raw stream (ddx-f8a11202).
492						// On error the artifact path is empty and the event body
493						// still contains the short verdict summary; callers of this
494						// loop can recover the full text from the reviewer session
495						// log if the artifact write failed.
496						artifactPath, artifactErr := persistReviewerStream(runtime.ProjectRoot, candidate.ID, report.AttemptID, reviewRes.RawOutput)
497						if artifactErr != nil && runtime.Log != nil {
498							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "reviewer stream artifact: %v\n", artifactErr)
499						}
500	
501						reviewSummary := EventBodySummary{
502							Harness:     reviewRes.ReviewerHarness,
503							Model:       reviewRes.ReviewerModel,
504							InputBytes:  reviewRes.InputBytes,
505							OutputBytes: reviewRes.OutputBytes,
506							ElapsedMS:   reviewRes.DurationMS,
507						}
508						switch reviewRes.Verdict {
509						case VerdictApprove:
510							// Approved: record the verdict event and then close.
511							// Closure must land AFTER the review event so the
512							// gate (ddx-e30e60a9) sees the terminal verdict.
513							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
514								Kind:      "review",
515								Summary:   "APPROVE",
516								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("APPROVE", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
517								Actor:     assignee,
518								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
519								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
520							})
521							if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); cerr != nil {
522								return result, cerr
523							}
524						case VerdictRequestChanges:
525							// Needs fixes: record the review verdict, then reopen
526							// with findings in notes. The review event must land
527							// even on non-approve paths so review-outcomes can
528							// attribute the rejection to the originating tier.
529							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
530								Kind:      "review",
531								Summary:   "REQUEST_CHANGES",
532								Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("REQUEST_CHANGES", reviewRes.Rationale, artifactPath), reviewSummary),
533								Actor:     assignee,
534								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
535								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
536							})
537							reopenNotes := reviewRes.Rationale
538							if reopenNotes == "" {
539								reopenNotes = reviewRes.RawOutput
540							}
541							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: REQUEST_CHANGES", reopenNotes); reopenErr != nil {
542								return result, reopenErr
543							}
544							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
545							report.Detail = "post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
546							reviewApproved = false
547						case VerdictBlock:
548							rationale := strings.TrimSpace(reviewRes.Rationale)
549							if rationale == "" {
550								_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
551									Kind:      "review-malfunction",
552									Summary:   "BLOCK without rationale",
553									Body:      appendEventSummary(reviewEventBody("BLOCK without rationale", "", artifactPath), reviewSummary),
554									Actor:     assignee,
555									Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
556									CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
557								})
558								report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
559								report.Detail = "post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)"
560								report.ReviewRationale = ""
561								reviewApproved = false
562								break
563							}
564							// Cannot proceed: record the verdict, then reopen and
565							// flag for human with BLOCK marker plus actionable rationale.
566							_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
567								Kind:      "review",
568								Summary:   "BLOCK",
569								Body:      appendEventSummary(rationale, reviewSummary),
570								Actor:     assignee,
571								Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
572								CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
573							})
574							blockNotes := "REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n" + rationale
575							if reopenErr := w.Store.Reopen(candidate.ID, "review: BLOCK", blockNotes); reopenErr != nil {
576								return result, reopenErr
577							}
578							report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
579							report.Detail = "post-merge review: BLOCK (flagged for human)"
580							reviewApproved = false
581						}
582					}
583				}
584	
585				if reviewApproved {
586					result.Successes++
587					result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
588				} else {
589					result.Failures++
590					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
591				}
592			} else {
593				if err := w.Store.Unclaim(candidate.ID); err != nil {
594					return result, err
595				}
596				if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
597					count, cerr := w.Store.IncrNoChangesCount(candidate.ID)
598					if cerr != nil {
599						return result, cerr
600					}
601					satisfied, evidence, aerr := w.adjudicateNoChanges(ctx, candidate.ID, count, maxNoChangesBeforeClose, report.NoChangesRationale, candidate.Acceptance, runtime.ProjectRoot)
602					if aerr != nil {
603						return result, aerr
604					}
605					if satisfied {
606						// Adjudication confirmed bead is already satisfied.
607						// Set the terminal status BEFORE the close so the late
608						// executeBeadLoopEvent append captures "already_satisfied"
609						// (not "no_changes"), and emit an early execute-bead
610						// evidence event so the closure gate accepts even when
611						// BaseRev is empty (test fixtures and genuinely-no-commit
612						// satisfied beads).
613						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied
614						if evidence != "" {
615							// Checker evidence explains why the bead is being closed;
616							// it takes precedence over the executor's attempt detail.
617							report.Detail = evidence
618						}
619						_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC()))
620						if cerr := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.BaseRev); cerr != nil {
621							return result, cerr
622						}
623						result.Successes++
624						result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()
625					} else {
626						// Unresolved: suppress immediate retry so the queue can
627						// move on to other beads.
628						if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
629							retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
630							if cerr := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); cerr != nil {
631								return result, cerr
632							}
633							report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
634						}
635						result.Failures++
636						result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
637					}
638				} else if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed {
639					// Push failed after a successful local merge/ff. The bead is
640					// NOT closed (commits live only locally) — park it long enough
641					// that subsequent loop iterations refuse to re-pick it until
642					// an operator clears the state. The push stderr in
643					// report.Detail is recorded as last_detail so it surfaces on
644					// the bead and on any direct claim attempt.
645					parkUntil := now().UTC().Add(365 * 24 * time.Hour)
646					if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, parkUntil, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
647						return result, err
648					}
649					report.RetryAfter = parkUntil.Format(time.RFC3339)
650					result.Failures++
651					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
652				} else {
653					if shouldSuppressNoProgress(report) {
654						retryAfter := now().UTC().Add(noProgressCooldown)
655						if err := w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(candidate.ID, retryAfter, report.Status, report.Detail); err != nil {
656							return result, err
657						}
658						report.RetryAfter = retryAfter.Format(time.RFC3339)
659					}
660					result.Failures++
661					result.LastFailureStatus = report.Status
662				}
663			}
664	
665			result.Results = append(result.Results, report)
666	
667			// Skip the late execute-bead append for already-satisfied beads —
668			// the satisfied path appends its own terminal event before
669			// CloseWithEvidence so the closure gate sees execution evidence.
670			// Duplicating it here would yield two identical events.
671			if report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
672				if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
673					return result, err
674				}
675			}
676	
677			// Emit terminal progress phase event.
678			terminalPhase := "failed"
679			if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess || report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied {
680				terminalPhase = "done"
681			} else if report.PreserveRef != "" {
682				terminalPhase = "preserved"
683			}
684			// Use the real attempt_id from the report if available.
685			finalAttemptID := report.AttemptID
686			if finalAttemptID == "" {
687				finalAttemptID = provAttemptID
688			}
689			phaseSeq++
690			emitProgress(runtime.ProgressCh, ProgressEvent{
691				EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
692				AttemptID: finalAttemptID,
693				WorkerID:  runtime.WorkerID,
694				ProjectID: runtime.ProjectRoot,
695				BeadID:    candidate.ID,
696				Harness:   harness,
697				Model:     model,
698				Profile:   profile,
699				Phase:     terminalPhase,
700				PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
701				Heartbeat: false,
702				TS:        now().UTC(),
703				ElapsedMS: now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
704				Message:   report.Detail,
705			})
706	
707			emit("bead.result", map[string]any{
708				"bead_id":              candidate.ID,
709				"status":               report.Status,
710				"detail":               report.Detail,
711				"session_id":           report.SessionID,
712				"result_rev":           report.ResultRev,
713				"base_rev":             report.BaseRev,
714				"preserve_ref":         report.PreserveRef,
715				"no_changes_rationale": report.NoChangesRationale,
716				"duration_ms":          now().Sub(runStart).Milliseconds(),
717			})
718	
719			if runtime.Log != nil {
720				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "✓ %s → %s\n", candidate.ID, formatLoopResult(report))
721			}
722	
723			if runtime.Once {
724				return result, nil
725			}
726		}
727	}

729	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) nextCandidate(attempted map[string]struct{}, labelFilter string) (bead.Bead, bool, error) {
730		ready, err := w.Store.ReadyExecution()
731		if err != nil {
732			return bead.Bead{}, false, err
733		}
734		for _, candidate := range ready {
735			if _, seen := attempted[candidate.ID]; seen {
736				continue
737			}
738			if labelFilter != "" && !HasBeadLabel(candidate.Labels, labelFilter) {
739				continue
740			}
741			return candidate, true, nil
742		}
743		return bead.Bead{}, false, nil
744	}
745	
746	// appendLoopRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence event on the bead
747	// from the executor's ExecuteBeadReport, so that review-outcomes analytics can
748	// attribute a subsequent review verdict to the originating provider/model tier.
749	// Best-effort: errors and missing-provider cases are silently ignored.
750	func appendLoopRoutingEvidence(store BeadEventAppender, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport, createdAt time.Time) {
751		if store == nil || beadID == "" {
752			return
753		}
754		provider := report.Provider
755		if provider == "" {
756			provider = report.Harness
757		}
758		if provider == "" {
759			return
760		}
761		body, err := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
762			"resolved_provider": provider,
763			"resolved_model":    report.Model,
764			"fallback_chain":    []string{},
765			"requested_profile": report.RequestedProfile,
766			"requested_tier":    report.RequestedTier,
767			"resolved_tier":     report.ResolvedTier,
768			"escalation_count":  report.EscalationCount,
769			"final_tier":        report.FinalTier,
770		})
771		if err != nil {
772			return
773		}
774		summary := "provider=" + provider
775		if report.Model != "" {
776			summary += " model=" + report.Model
777		}
778		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
779			Kind:      "routing",
780			Summary:   summary,
781			Body:      string(body),
782			Actor:     "ddx",
783			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
784			CreatedAt: createdAt,
785		})
786	}
787	
788	func executeBeadLoopEvent(report ExecuteBeadReport, actor string, createdAt time.Time) bead.BeadEvent {
789		parts := []string{}
790		if report.Detail != "" {
791			parts = append(parts, report.Detail)
792		}
793		if report.Tier != "" {
794			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s", report.Tier))
795		}
796		if report.ProbeResult != "" {
797			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("probe_result=%s", report.ProbeResult))
798		}
799		if report.NoChangesRationale != "" {
800			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("rationale: %s", report.NoChangesRationale))
801		}
802		if report.ReviewRationale != "" {
803			parts = append(parts, report.ReviewRationale)
804		}
805		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
806			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("preserve_ref=%s", report.PreserveRef))
807		}
808		if report.ResultRev != "" {
809			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("result_rev=%s", report.ResultRev))
810		}
811		if report.BaseRev != "" {
812			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("base_rev=%s", report.BaseRev))
813		}
814		if report.RetryAfter != "" {
815			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("retry_after=%s", report.RetryAfter))
816		}
817	
818		return bead.BeadEvent{
819			Kind:      "execute-bead",
820			Summary:   report.Status,
821			Body:      strings.Join(parts, "\n"),
822			Actor:     actor,
823			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
824			CreatedAt: createdAt,
825		}
826	}

828	// writeLoopEvent emits one structured JSONL line to sink describing a

905	// adjudicateNoChanges runs the no-change adjudication step for a bead.
906	// It returns (satisfied, evidence, err). When satisfied is true the bead
907	// should be closed as already_satisfied with the evidence string. When false
908	// retry suppression (cooldown) should be applied and the bead left open.
909	//
910	// If a SatisfactionChecker is configured it is called first. Otherwise:
911	//   - When the report carries a specific rationale (cites a commit SHA or test
912	//     name), the bead is closed as already_satisfied on the first occurrence.
913	//   - Otherwise the default count-based rule applies (close after maxNoChangesBeforeClose).
914	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) adjudicateNoChanges(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount, maxNoChangesBeforeClose int, rationale, acceptance, projectRoot string) (bool, string, error) {
915		if w.SatisfactionChecker != nil {
916			return w.SatisfactionChecker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
917		}
918		candidate := false
919		evidence := ""
920		switch {
921		case rationaleIsSpecific(rationale):
922			candidate = true
923			evidence = rationale
924		case noChangesCount >= maxNoChangesBeforeClose:
925			candidate = true
926			evidence = fmt.Sprintf("no_changes on %d consecutive attempt(s); bead treated as already satisfied", noChangesCount)
927		}
928		if !candidate {
929			return false, "", nil
930		}
931		// Tighten the gate: when AC names structural properties (test functions,
932		// deleted files, removed struct fields), refuse already_satisfied unless
933		// each property holds in the worktree / rationale. Prevents false closes
934		// where a regression suite passes but the AC's specific contract is unmet.
935		if claims := ParseACClaims(acceptance); len(claims) > 0 {
936			if ok, why := VerifyACClaims(claims, projectRoot, rationale); !ok {
937				return false, why, nil
938			}
939		}
940		return true, evidence, nil
941	}

950	// classifyReviewError maps a reviewer error and partial result into one of the
951	// four FEAT-022 §12 taxonomy classes. Resolution order:
952	//  1. reviewRes.Error if it's already one of the canonical class identifiers
953	//     (the reviewer set it explicitly — preferred path).
954	//  2. The error string text (for backwards-compatible reviewers that only
955	//     embed the class in their message).
956	//  3. ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a fallback when the strict-parse error
957	//     leaks through a reviewer that did not set Error.
958	//  4. Default to transport (network/provider-side failure) when nothing else
959	//     matches.
960	func classifyReviewError(reviewErr error, reviewRes *ReviewResult) string {
961		if reviewRes != nil {
962			switch reviewRes.Error {
963			case evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
964				evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty,
965				evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable,
966				evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport:
967				return reviewRes.Error
968			}
969		}
970		msg := ""
971		if reviewErr != nil {
972			msg = reviewErr.Error()
973		}
974		switch {
975		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow):
976			return evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow
977		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty):
978			return evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
979		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable):
980			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
981		case strings.Contains(msg, evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport):
982			return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
983		case reviewErr != nil && strings.Contains(msg, ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable.Error()):
984			return evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
985		}
986		return evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport
987	}

989	// reResultRevField extracts the result_rev value from a structured review-error
990	// event body (one `result_rev=<sha>` line per event, written by
991	// reviewErrorEventBody). The format is intentionally line-oriented so it
992	// survives the AppendEvent body cap without losing the rev association.
993	var reResultRevField = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^result_rev=([^\s]+)\s*$`)
994	
995	// countPriorReviewErrors returns the number of `review-error` events already
996	// recorded against this bead whose body cites the given result_rev. This is
997	// the FEAT-022 §14 retry counter — it is event-scoped (no separate counter
998	// field on the bead) so a fresh result_rev naturally resets the count.
999	func countPriorReviewErrors(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) int {
1000		if resultRev == "" {
1001			return 0
1002		}
1003		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
1004		if err != nil {
1005			return 0
1006		}
1007		n := 0
1008		for _, ev := range events {
1009			if ev.Kind != "review-error" {
1010				continue
1011			}
1012			m := reResultRevField.FindStringSubmatch(ev.Body)
1013			if m == nil {
1014				continue
1015			}
1016			if m[1] == resultRev {
1017				n++
1018			}
1019		}
1020		return n
1021	}
1022	
1023	// reviewErrorEventBody is the canonical body shape for review-error and
1024	// review-manual-required events. It carries the failure class, attempt count,
1025	// and result_rev as discrete lines so operators can grep without parsing the
1026	// full reviewer error text. The trailing free-form message is the raw
1027	// reviewer-error string for forensics.
1028	func reviewErrorEventBody(class string, attemptCount int, resultRev, message string) string {
1029		var b strings.Builder
1030		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "failure_class=%s\n", class)
1031		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "attempt_count=%d\n", attemptCount)
1032		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "result_rev=%s\n", resultRev)
1033		if message != "" {
1034			b.WriteString("\n")
1035			b.WriteString(message)
1036		}
1037		return b.String()
1038	}

1040	func sleepWithContext(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error {
1041		if d <= 0 {
1042			return nil
1043		}
1044		timer := time.NewTimer(d)
1045		defer timer.Stop()
1046		select {
1047		case <-ctx.Done():
1048			return ctx.Err()
1049		case <-timer.C:
1050			return nil
1051		}
1052	}

3	import "strings"

5	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
6	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
7	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
8	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
9	// success, so the bead stays open.
10	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
11	
12	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
13	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
14	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
15	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
16	const (
17		FailureModeContextOverflow = "context_overflow"
18		FailureModeMergeConflict   = "merge_conflict"
19		FailureModeTestFailure     = "test_failure"
20		FailureModeBuildFailure    = "build_failure"
21		FailureModeTimeout         = "timeout"
22		FailureModeAuthError       = "auth_error"
23		FailureModeNoChanges       = "no_changes"
24		FailureModeRatchetMiss     = "ratchet_miss"
25		FailureModeUnknown         = "unknown"
26	)
27	
28	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
29	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
30	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
31	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
32	//
33	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
34	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
35	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
36	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
37	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
38	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
39		switch outcome {
40		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
41			if exitCode == 0 {
42				return ""
43			}
44			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
45			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
46		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
47			return FailureModeNoChanges
48		}
49	
50		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
51		switch {
52		case containsAny(lower,
53			"context length",
54			"context_length_exceeded",
55			"context window",
56			"maximum context",
57			"prompt is too long",
58			"prompt too long",
59			"token limit",
60			"too many tokens",
61			"exceeds the context"):
62			return FailureModeContextOverflow
63		case containsAny(lower,
64			"unauthorized",
65			"401 unauthorized",
66			"invalid api key",
67			"invalid_api_key",
68			"authentication failed",
69			"authentication_error",
70			"no api key",
71			"missing api key",
72			"quota exceeded",
73			"insufficient quota",
74			"insufficient_quota",
75			"rate limit",
76			"rate_limit",
77			"429"):
78			return FailureModeAuthError
79		case containsAny(lower,
80			"timeout",
81			"timed out",
82			"context deadline exceeded",
83			"deadline exceeded",
84			"signal: killed"):
85			return FailureModeTimeout
86		case containsAny(lower,
87			"merge conflict",
88			"ff-merge not possible",
89			"merge failed",
90			"automatic merge failed"):
91			return FailureModeMergeConflict
92		case containsAny(lower,
93			"build failed",
94			"compilation failed",
95			"compile error",
96			"compilation error",
97			"cannot find package",
98			"undefined:",
99			"undefined reference",
100			"syntax error"):
101			return FailureModeBuildFailure
102		case containsAny(lower,
103			"test failed",
104			"tests failed",
105			"--- fail:",
106			"assertion failed",
107			"test failure"):
108			return FailureModeTestFailure
109		}
110		if exitCode != 0 {
111			return FailureModeUnknown
112		}
113		return ""
114	}

116	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
117	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
118	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
119		for _, n := range needles {
120			if n == "" {
121				continue
122			}
123			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
124				return true
125			}
126		}
127		return false
128	}

130	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
131	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
132	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
133	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
134	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
135	// bead merged cleanly.
136	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
137		switch landingOutcome {
138		case "merged":
139			return ""
140		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
141			return FailureModeNoChanges
142		case "error":
143			if workerMode != "" {
144				return workerMode
145			}
146			return FailureModeUnknown
147		case "preserved":
148			switch landingReason {
149			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
150				return FailureModeMergeConflict
151			case "post-run checks failed":
152				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
153			case RatchetPreserveReason:
154				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
155			}
156			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
157			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
158			if workerMode != "" {
159				return workerMode
160			}
161			return FailureModeUnknown
162		}
163		if workerMode != "" {
164			return workerMode
165		}
166		return FailureModeUnknown
167	}

169	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
170	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
171	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
172	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
173	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
174	// test-oriented by convention.
175	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
176		for _, gr := range gateResults {
177			if gr.Status != "fail" {
178				continue
179			}
180			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
181			if containsAny(combined,
182				"build failed",
183				"compilation failed",
184				"compile error",
185				"compilation error",
186				"cannot find package",
187				"undefined:",
188				"undefined reference",
189				"syntax error") {
190				return FailureModeBuildFailure
191			}
192		}
193		return FailureModeTestFailure
194	}

196	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
197	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
198	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
199	const (
200		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded = "task_succeeded"
201		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed    = "task_failed"
202		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges = "task_no_changes"

206	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
207	const (
208		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
209		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
210		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
211		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
212		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
213		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
214		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
215		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
216		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
217	
218		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
219		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
220		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
221		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
222		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
223	)

225	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
226	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
227	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
228	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
229		if exitCode != 0 {
230			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
231		}
232	
233		switch outcome {
234		case "merged":
235			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
236			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
237			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
238			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
239			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
240			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
241				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
242			}
243			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
244		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
245			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
246		case "error":
247			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
248		case "preserved":
249			switch reason {
250			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
251				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
252			case "post-run checks failed":
253				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
254			case RatchetPreserveReason:
255				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
256			default:
257				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
258				// explicitly requested no merge.
259				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
260			}
261		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
262			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
263		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
264			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
265		default:
266			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
267		}
268	}

270	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
271		switch {
272		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
273			return reason + ": " + errMsg
274		case reason != "":
275			return reason
276		case errMsg != "":
277			return errMsg
278		case status != "":
279			return status
280		default:
281			return "unknown"
282		}
283	}

105	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
106	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
107		var ids []string
108		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
109			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
110		}

112	}

114	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
115	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
116		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
117			if d.DependsOnID == id {
118				return true
119			}
120		}
121		return false
122	}

124	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
125	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
126		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
127			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
128				return // already exists
129			}
130		}
131		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
132			IssueID:     b.ID,
133			DependsOnID: depID,
134			Type:        depType,
135			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
136		})
137	}

140	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
141		var filtered []Dependency

143			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
144				filtered = append(filtered, d)
145			}
146		}
147		b.Dependencies = filtered
148	}

3	All notable changes to DDx are documented in this file.
4	
5	## [Unreleased]
6	
7	### Routing point release — ddx-agent v0.9.23
8	
9	DDx now consumes the cumulative routing contract delivered upstream across
10	`github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` v0.9.10..v0.9.24 (pinned at v0.9.23 in
11	`cli/go.mod`). Upstream shipped these capabilities incrementally across
12	multiple tags rather than as a single release, so this entry bundles the
13	combined contract DDx now relies on.
14	
15	Consumed upstream capabilities (commit hash · upstream bead):
16	
17	- **Cost-aware routing tiebreak** — `b85e90e` · [agent-0dafc7f0](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl) (regression coverage tightened in `9c80eb1`). Adds cost as a routing dimension so equally-capable candidates resolve to the cheaper provider.
18	- **First-class routing profiles** — `b645280` · [agent-191a74f9](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Ships the `default`, `local`, and `standard` profiles with zero-interaction defaults.
19	- **Routing reference docs** — `faafff2`, `db26108` · [agent-1f46cf22](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Publishes the routing reference and clarifies the best-provider cost baseline.
20	- **Public route decision trace** — `0bc2bf4` · [agent-53f38d95](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Exposes the candidate decision trace so callers can inspect why a route was chosen.
21	- **Supported-models allow-list** — `fd71e00` · [agent-bab52778](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Gates exact pins by the harness allow-list so unsupported models fail fast.
22	- **Typed explicit-pin errors** — `5992c7b` · [agent-dfabb10b](~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl). Returns typed errors for explicit-pin routing failures instead of opaque strings.
23	
24	The combined contract DDx consumes:
25	
26	- **Zero-interaction defaults** via the `default` profile.
27	- **Profiles** (`default`, `local`, `standard`) selectable without bespoke config.
28	- **Override semantics** with explicit pins gated by the supported-models allow-list and surfaced through typed errors.
29	- **Public decision trace** for inspecting candidate selection.
30	- **Cost dimension** as a tiebreak in routing decisions.
31	
32	This entry, together with the `cli/go.mod` pin at v0.9.23, satisfies AC #1 of
33	parent bead `ddx-fdd3ea36` — there is no single upstream release tag that
34	bundles the routing point release, so traceability lives here.

39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
64	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
65	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
66	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
67	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
68	
69	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
70	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
71	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
72			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
73	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
74	
75	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
76	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main

100	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
101		beadID := args[0]
102		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
103			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
104		}
105		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
106		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
107		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
108		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
109		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
110		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
111		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
112		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
113		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
114		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
115		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
116	
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{
120			Harness:       harness,
121			Model:         model,
122			Provider:      provider,
123			ModelRef:      modelRef,
124			Effort:        effort,
125			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
126		})
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the execute-loop preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
157		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
158		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
159		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
160		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
161			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
162				return err
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
167		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
168		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
169	
170		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
171		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
172		if err != nil && res == nil {
173			return err
174		}
175	
176		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
177		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
178		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
179		//
180		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
181		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
182		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
183		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
184		if res != nil {
185			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
186			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
187				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
188			} else {
189				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
190				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
191					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
192				}
193			}
194			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
195				NoMerge:         noMerge,
196				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
197			}
198	
199			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
200			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
201			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
202			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
203			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
204			// duration of gate evaluation.
205			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
206				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
207				if ctxErr != nil {
208					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
209					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
210				} else if wt != "" {
211					defer cleanup()
212					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
213					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
214					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
215					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
216				}
217			}
218	
219			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
220				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
221			} else if err == nil {
222				err = landErr
223			}
224		}
225	
226		if err != nil {
227			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
228			return err
229		}
230	
231		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
232	}
233	
234	func writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd *cobra.Command, res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult, asJSON bool) error {
235		if res == nil {
236			return nil
237		}
238		if asJSON {
239			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
240			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
241			return enc.Encode(res)
242		}
243	
244		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "bead:    %s\n", res.BeadID)
245		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "base:    %s\n", res.BaseRev)
246		if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
247			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "result:  %s\n", res.ResultRev)
248		}
249		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "outcome: %s\n", res.Outcome)
250		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "status:  %s\n", res.Status)
251		if res.Detail != "" {
252			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "detail:  %s\n", res.Detail)
253		}
254		if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
255			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "rationale: %s\n", res.NoChangesRationale)
256		}
257		if res.PreserveRef != "" {
258			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "ref:     %s\n", res.PreserveRef)
259		}
260		return nil
261	}

## [Unreleased]

### execute-loop: structured `declined_needs_decomposition` outcome

Adds a first-class outcome distinct from `no_changes` and `execution_failed`
for executors that conclude a bead is too large to deliver in one pass and
must first be split into sub-beads. Previously this case was conveyed via
free-form text in `no_changes_rationale`, and the loop kept re-attempting the
bead under a short cooldown — burning tokens to re-derive the same
recommendation across runs.

The new contract:

- `ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition` (`declined_needs_decomposition`)
  is a new value of the execute-bead status. Executors set it together with
  `DecompositionRecommendation []string` (recommended sub-bead titles) and
  optional `DecompositionRationale string` on `ExecuteBeadReport`.
- The execute-loop responds by appending a structured
  `decomposition-recommendation` event (JSON body carrying the rationale and
  recommended sub-beads) and parking the bead with a 365-day cooldown so
  subsequent loop iterations do not re-attempt it. The bead remains open;
  its status field is unchanged.
- A first-class CLI for the cooldown surface lands as `ddx bead cooldown
  show <id>` and `ddx bead cooldown clear <id>`. Operators should reach for
  these commands instead of editing the magic `execute-loop-retry-after`
  Extra key. The existing `ddx bead update --set/--unset
  execute-loop-retry-after=...` workflow continues to work as a power-user
  override but is deprecated for the decomposition case — clearing a
  cooldown via the new command is the intended path.

Migration: this is an additive contract. Executors that do not emit the new
status see no behaviour change. Existing cooldown workflows (no_changes
short cooldown, push_failed long park) are unchanged.

Regression coverage:
`TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead` in
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go`.

data.text:
Committed as `118dfeb`. The pre-commit hook flagged pre-existing test failures (installation tests needing a binary, an unrelated golden file mismatch, quorum config) that exist on the base commit too — my changes don't touch those areas.

Summary:
- New status `ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition` + structured `DecompositionRecommendation`/`DecompositionRationale` fields on `ExecuteBeadReport` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go, execute_bead_loop.go).
- Loop branch parks the bead 365 days and appends a JSON-bodied `decomposition-recommendation` event (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go).
- New `ddx bead cooldown show|clear` subcommand (cli/cmd/bead.go).
- Regression test `TestExecuteBeadWorkerDeclinedNeedsDecompositionParksBead` (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go).
- CHANGELOG entry under [Unreleased].
